entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Data Migration Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Karthick

nice blog, one comment to your statement:

"The uploaded template file must be less than 100 MB in size. If your data is too large, consider splitting it into multiple files."

You can also use ZIP files. To avoid splitting in multiple files you can also try to use CSV files instead of the XML files. Using CSV files, you can easily tailor your files and only need to use the files for the structures and fields you want to process. A CSV file has much less administrative data than an XML file. The 100 MB limit is the same, but with tailored CSV files this http-protocol related size limit is not reached that fast as using XML files.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Optional/Required field change via Migration Object M...
entry.content:
Hello Naresha,

that message ID MM 85 is thrown by the API used to migrate the data. That error message has nothing to do with the visibility definition of the migration template. Within the migration template you will only see fields being marked as mandatory that are always mandatory- Fields that can be maintained mandatory or not mandatory via business process configuration or fields that are dependent on field value combinations mandatory or not mandatory are never marked as mandatory within the migration template. Each sheet/structure of the migration template has an assigned database table. Setting a field Mandatory or not is done via database constraints. Changing database constraints dynamically is not possible. 

For the API the material group is a mandatory field. You must either verify if you can configure this field within the different business process settings for material master to be not mandatory or you must provide a value for that field. 
You can also check the KBA 2756835 - Material Group MARA-MATKL is mandatory for MM02 despite set as optional - SAP for Me
There it is explained that if data for purchasing view is being used, material group is always mandatory.

BR
Frank

entry.title: Re: Atualização data rejeição NFe em Homologação
entry.content:
Oi, Alan,

Obrigado pela observação e correção. É DRC mesmo e me atrapalhei aqui na revisão!

Vai nos ajudar bastante mesmo, principalmente a manter a pressão cardiáca e stress controlados.

Agora entendi o motivo da SAP ter rejeitado meu post no WorkZone da Reforma Tributária.

entry.title: Re: Is it Possible to Show only India Map in SAC stories...
entry.content: Thanks MKreitein for your response. The dots are in India only. But with setting to a certain zoom factor also, other neighbouring countries are visible. Is it possible only legends for India is visible and not for other countries.?

entry.title: ACR - MX LOCALIZATION - Missing Reporting Activities ID or Report Activity ID
entry.content:
Hi community 

We are trying to implement ACR for MX Localization

After BC set activation we notice there is missing "activity ID" from the FIORI side (even thought exist on the maintenance table SRFVC_RPG_ENT)

Therefore, We cannot generate the reports

How can input the missing entries?

If I choose "add" from the FIORI side it ask for a Z Custom Object, so I do not think is the right solution

Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: Subject of Service now ticket created from Cloud ALM Job Monitoring does not comes with Job name
entry.content:
After configuring job monitoring in Cloud ALM and creating the integration with Service Now in order to get automatic ticket for created alerts the tickets are created in Service Now, but the ticket subject doesn't contain the Job name.
How to create a ticket with the Job Name in the subject?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Subject of Service now ticket created from Cloud ALM...
entry.content:
Hello, 

The built-in mapping for Service now does not include the job name in the subject of the ticket.
In order to include the required fields kindly review and adjust the mapping as per instructed in "Create Mapping" section of Integration with External Ticket Systems.
The available fields for usage are described in https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/apis/event-situations

In order to include the job name in the subject you may include one of the following fields:
-tags.subject -> Event name + Job name
-tags.NAME -> Job name

Best Regards

entry.title: Story Report for Goal Management
entry.content:
Hello,

I am creating story report for Goal Management, upon adding the template name, it is coming as blank in output, kindly suggest how to rectify it.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: What tips would you give an organisation planning to...
entry.content: Always try to see the bigger picture, try to push for clean core as the system which you are going to build will stay for longer run.

entry.title: Re: SAP MM
entry.content: This is custom message, and I believe you are performing FUT, in this case you need to connect with Tech team as validation is not maintained correctly.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere - Copy objects between Spaces within the s...
entry.content: Hi Hiri, seems the connection was not changed in the JSON. All occurrences need to be replaced. This works. Check what object is having the warning, is it data / replication flow or model? Cheers, Martin

entry.title: Re: Custom field and logics
entry.content:
Hello Fatima,

have you checked the customer migration object's documentation (custom field section)?

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/b251af240d9c41e9b9baf94603b9491a.html

the business context is assigned 

 

Business Context Structure

Master Data: Business Partner (BP_CUSTVEND1)General Data (S_CUST_GEN)
Customer Core View (CUSTOMER_GENERAL)General Data (S_CUST_GEN)
Customer company code core (CUST_COMPANYCODE)Company Data (S_CUST_COMPANY)
Partner Sales area core view (CUST_SALES)Sales Data (S_CUST_SALES_DATA)

Each business context is assigned to an API structure and that API structure is assigned to a sender structure within the migration object. There is no API structure existing for Key-user-extensibility support for tax data.
The business context Customerer Core View (Customer General) is assigned to S_CUST_GEN (General Data) structure. Fields within that business context are only visible in that structure. They cannot be made visible in another structure than the one mentioned within the documentation.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: data action to copy data by version and date from cla...
entry.content:
@Raj4 

Its difficult to provide a working code without model knowledge. But LINK syntax is something like below

DATA() = LINK([AccountModel], [d/Version] = "Plan01",  [d/Entity] = "REG0001",[d/Product] = "PRD0002", [d/Customer] = "CUST0001")

You can refer to the syntax here

Change Values in Advanced Formulas with DATA, RESULTLOOKUP, and LINK | SAP Help Portal 

 

Hope this helps

Nikhil

entry.title: Employment Status in Story reports
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to filter out the inactive employees from one of my story reports, but the employment status = "Inactive" option under Job Information schema is not available

Additionally, the "Status" field under Basic User Information schema  is greyed out.

kindly suggest how to achieve it.

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Text is not visible in lower level of level based hie...
entry.content:
Hi Shlomi, I kind of missed the info, that you have compounded key in your original post. 

You need to switch to Hierarchy with Directory. That supports compounded keys

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products and SAP BusinessObjects: Similar Purpose, Different Approa
entry.content: Is list of predefined bdc data products published anywhere?

entry.title: Can the flavor builder role be limited in scope?
entry.content:
Hello community,

Is there a way to grant a user the flavor builder role (/PERSONAS/EDITOR_ROLE) but restrict the user from using parts of the editor (for example the scripting editor)?

Kindly,

Cristin

entry.title: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content: Mass printing of invoices from Adobe, using a custom-developed program, is getting stuck on these invoice printing processes (approximately 12,000 per day). Do you recommend setting up a local server for Adobe or setting it up on AWS? Which is more efficient and secure?

entry.title: SAP UI5 version 1.71.69 will expire in Q3/2025. Will it expire before Q3 or at end of Q3/2025?
entry.content:
Hi,

It is mentioned in the website that SAP UI5 version 1.71.69 will expire in Q3/2025. Will it expire before the start of Q3 or at the end of Q3/2025? When do we need to update the version? Can someone please tell me.

entry.title: Re: HTML5 Apps launched form SAP build workzone site are not opening
entry.content: There is no backend system involved. Its just static content

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content:
Nog entirely sure if by AWS you mean hosting then (otherwise local) server on that hyperscaler, or doing it on BTP but a little over 15 euro a day is a steal.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/forms-service-by-adobe?region=all&tab=service_plan

entry.title: Re: Issue with recording completions for multiple Content Objects in a single Item
entry.content: We are having the same issue and we are using SCORM 2004 4Ed.

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content: Hi MatthiasL Thanks for the reply. When I refer to AWS, I mean installing it on the AWS server where we already have other servers. Today, we have a dedicated local server for Adobe services.I didn't understand, via BTP?

entry.title: Re: Adding Additional Quantity Fields in Account Based CO-PA
entry.content: I have an issue when running the report Market Segment - Actuals where it is not summing the amount in Additional Quantity 1 and only return "*" in the totals.  Any idea how to fix this?

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 version 1.71.69 will expire in Q3/2025. Will...
entry.content:
It means the end of the quarter. So on October 1st 2025 you should have it updated already. If you don't do it and use WorkZone, all apps will break in DEV + TEST + PROD at the same day and time.

As patches usually last 1 years ideally you should upgrade them every six months or so.

entry.title: Re: Printing Adobe forms via local server or AWS
entry.content: SAP BTP has its own, hosted, Adobe Forms printing service. Basically enable it in BTP, link a RFC to the Cloud application and some other configuration, and you are printing all kinds of Adobe Forms by a specific SaaS application for SAP. More info on link above.

entry.title: How to set up a new start condition (WERKS) for the flexible workflow for purchase orders?
entry.content:
We need to create a new WORKFLOW start condition in SAP Public. Currently, we only have

 

Thank you,

entry.title: Re: My SAP Sapphire Orlando experience
entry.content:
Awesome post, Brian! Thank you for the warm introduction. I had the pleasure of attending my first SAP Sapphire this year, and your leadership and guidance onsite made a huge difference in helping me navigate the event and maximize the experience—for both myself and our customers.

I also had the opportunity to showcase SAP Sales and Service Cloud solutions at one of our CX booths, and I was struck by how energized and engaged our customers were—especially around the potential of AI to transform service operations. That enthusiasm really echoed what we’re seeing in the field: organizations are eager to streamline their service journeys, and they’re turning to SAP for intelligent, integrated solutions to make it happen.

The excitement around AI agents like the Case Classification Agent and Knowledge Creation Agent was palpable. I had several conversations that reinforced your key takeaway—AI and integration aren’t just trends; they’re becoming foundational to how enterprises deliver value.

Looking forward to continuing the conversation and learning from this community!

 

Brendan Del Biondo demonstrating key features of Sales Cloud at Sapphire 2025

entry.title: IDOC xsd import giving error in message mapping in SAP CPI iflow
entry.content:
Hi experts,
I am trying to upload the IDOC xsd (from PO ->IDOC ->xsd) but i am getting the error on attributes and also on idoc control segment field
Reference - IDOC xsd import error.png
 
I then came across the below post
CPI Message Mapping XSD Error
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/cpi-message-mapping-xsd-error/qaq-p/12048997
 
I tried to follow the steps as suggested -
Downloaded the XML Schema at http://www.w3.org/2001/xml.xsd and uploaded it as a resource to the iflow
the IDOC xsd does not have any reference and no import 
But tried it anyways which i think is the wrong location anyways and it gave error as expected -<xsd:schema xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" schemaLocation="xml.xsd"/>
 
I have searched and i am unable to find why an IDOC xsd generated from PO is not getting uploaded correctly
I can view it correctly and the xsd is valid
 
Please help me as i need to be able to upload the xsd for my message mapping

entry.title: Re: Which development installation should I choose?
entry.content: The only thing you need is the Crystal Reports Designer.

entry.title: Fiori App Controller Extension: Overrides not being triggered successfully
entry.content:
I'm trying to make a simple controller extension, which would hook onto a method when user clicks a button. The name of this method is "onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1."

I've tried the following code, and several variations, trying to get a simple console.log message, but I can't get any of the methods below to trigger. Is there something I'm missing in order to get any of the below methods to trigger after their respective base controller methods are called?

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/OverrideExecution'
    ],

   
    function (
        ControllerExtension,
        OverrideExecution //,Dialog, Button, mobileLibrary
       
    ) {
        console.log("testing 1");
        "use strict";
       
        return ControllerExtension.extend("customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs.PopupMessage", {
            metadata: {
                methods: {
                    onclick: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },

                    onmousedown: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },

                    onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },
                }
            },
            override: {
                onclick: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                },

                onmousedown: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                },
               
                onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                }
            }
        });
    }
)
 
 
 
Manifest: 
{
"fileName": "manifest",
"layer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
"fileType": "appdescr_variant",
"reference": "i2d.eam.perfmaintjobs.manages1",
"id": "customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs",
"namespace": "apps/i2d.eam.perfmaintjobs.manages1/appVariants/customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs/",
"version": "0.1.0",
"content": [
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "@i18n"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n|sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport|C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/ListReport/C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n|sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage|C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/ObjectPage/C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n|sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage|C_MaintenanceJobTP"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/ObjectPage/C_MaintenanceJobTP/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_setMinUI5Version",
"content": {
"minUI5Version": "1.108.38"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_app_setTitle",
"content": {},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
}
}
]
}

entry.title: Why purchasing group is created in material management configuration section instead of org. stru.
entry.content:
Hello All, 

As you all know that purchasing group is an organizational unit of the material management and also a floating org. unit, but when we create it we go to material management configuration path section instead of  organization structure (definition). The only answer I have thought about is because it has not assignment to any other org. unit. I want to make sure about this. I would appreciate anyone who can clarify this and thank you in advance. 
MM (Materials Management) 
MM Purchasing 
SAP Supply Chain Management 

SAP Community

entry.title: Physical quantity and available quantity in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can someone explain what is the difference between physical quantity and available quantity in SAP EWM?

entry.title: Re: Which development installation should I choose?

entry.content:
Hi Cristin,

Yes. You can create your own role(s) as described here.

entry.title: Re: Can the flavor builder role be limited in scope?

entry.title: Re: Blog it forward - third time's a charm
entry.content:
Nice blog! I like your list on famous people I've seen or met... Here's a few of mine...

  1. Gwyneth Paltrow - Near LA at a Brentwood restaurant
  2. Jeffry Lurie (Philadelphia Eagles Owner) - At a concert in the super box area
  3. Pete Rose (Most Hits in Major League Baseball) - At a concert backstage 
  4. Louis Ferrigno (Incredible Hulk) - Walking around LA
  5. Danny DeVito (Taxi, Matilda, It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia) in LA 
  6. Bill McDermott (Does that count? I'd say so!) - At a Fireside Chat which I co-produced!
  7. My daughter chatted with Larry David (Seinfeld, Curb Your Enthusiasm) recently...so I'll include that one as an extension! 🙂

I like your TV picks by the decade. I have too many to list at the moment! 
Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: wrong calculation Actual costing
entry.content:
Hello friends,

I have encountered a significant issue with Actual Costing at one of the companies that recently approached me. The problem is that the calculation of the product price in the Cumulative, Consumption, and Ending Inventory sections is incorrect. Specifically, the price of the product, which is derived from dividing the Actual Value by the quantity, is one value, but as we can see in the figure, the price in the Consumption and Ending Inventory sections is something entirely different.

Even the allocation of variances is completely wrong. The Price Difference share should be calculated as the total Price Difference divided by the Cumulative quantity, and this number should be multiplied by the amounts in Consumption and Ending Inventory to determine the share of each section. However, the system has not calculated it this way, and interestingly, the total variance has been fully distributed.

When I reviewed the calculations, I noticed that there is a long list of products in the program FCMLHELP_COMP_ACDOC_MLDOC_EXTR, and upon further investigation, it seems that this issue is due to data archiving. Now, I have a question: Is there any solution for this? I have spent a lot of time checking SAP Notes (SNotes) but couldn’t find anything relevant. Also, can the steps in FCMLHELP help in resolving this discrepancy?

entry.title: Re: What is the difference create user and create technic...
entry.content:
Adding some information on the topic.

At Authorization Concept for Technical Users | SAP Help Portal  the text is self explanatory.

SAP Technical Users

SAP technical users are a specific type of technical user available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. SAP predefines these users to perform operating tasks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: Blog it forward - third time's a charm
entry.content: @RichBlumberg of course Bill is famous to us at least! 🙂

entry.title: SAP Commerce Cloud framework details
entry.content:
Hi team, 

We would like to know more details about SAP Commerce Cloud framework, presented in each module and which release version is compatible with JDK 17. 

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud framework details
entry.content:
Hello, 

You can find about the framework and compatibility in the article Third-Party Compatibility, where you can browse through each SAP Commerce Cloud 2211 patch for more information. The article also shows that SAP Commerce Cloud 2211 is compatible with JDK 17.

Let me know in case you have additional questions on the topic!

entry.title: Re: Items not appearing in library despite being uploaded...
entry.content: Thank you for the pointers. The impacted system is not yet enabled for real-time search updates. Will check with SAP support .

entry.title: Re: Treating Business Goals as "Individual" Goals possible?
entry.content:
@mkrieg 

Billable Goal --> Team section of VarPay with own weight

Individual Goal(s) --> Individual section of VarPay with own weight

You'd have to find a way to bring over the Billable goal fields into a goal (from Goal Management) that then feeds the PM form.

Regarding weights, no we can't have a different split per employee (or you would have to create as many bonus plans as there are employees which is not recommended)... Aren't there guidelines around it ? It's hard for me to believe the company would let managers and HR decide for each employee whatever they want in terms of weight split.... If that's the case you'd have to do some customization and it will be a bit complex.

Also, on top of Billable Hours and Individual Goals is there a Corporate Goals section or not ? If so, how does that go into the weights ? 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Configuring Plant Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA Book written by Karl Liebstückel - 725 pages, PDF book
entry.content:
Hello, 

Can anyone please share the pdf version of Latest edition copy of Configuring Plant Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA written by Karl Liebstückel - 725 pages, PDF book

Configuring Plant Maintenance in SAP S/4HANA (Latest Edition) - 2024 by Rheinwerk Publishing Inc., Boston (MA)

Thanks 

Sravan

entry.title: Re: Use of element HEADER of CDS ENTITY SEPM_SDDL_SO_INVOICE_ITEM is not permitted.
entry.content:
check 

3387003 - The use of CDS Entity XYZ is not permitted - Developer Extensibility - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: Assigned a task list to an operation. TL contains a PRT. How do I show this PRT during confirmation
entry.content: I created a task list for an Equipment. In this task list, I added two PRTs that are actually measurement points for the equipment. Now, I want to see these PRTs during confirmation of the operation of the PM order. I don't see the PRT tab, how can I bring that and also how I can populate the PRT as Meas Points so that when confirmation is posted, the system creates Meas Docs.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Learning
entry.content:
Hi Tina,

Check this link

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/positioning-sap-business-technology-platform

Regards

entry.title: Enhanced FinOps Capabilities for SAP BTP
entry.content:
We're excited to announce that SAP BTP is introducing powerful new FinOps capabilities, to help you better manage, control and optimize your cloud spending. These capabilities (currently available as Beta features) focus on two critical areas:

  • Enhanced control of costs and consumption via Budget management with Alerts
  • Better planning and optimization of cloud credit with Forecasting

Stay in Control with Budgets and Alerts

The new Budget feature empowers global account admins with SAP BTP Enterprise Agreement (SAP BTPEA) or Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement (CPEA) or a combination of these consumption-based models with the subscription-based commercial model, to monitor both costs and service utilization metrics, enabling them to:

  • Set clear spending amounts at the global account level or for specific organizational units by selecting subaccounts and optionally choosing specific plans to monitor, enabling precise control over spending.
  • Define and manage service usage by setting usage amounts for specific service plans and monitoring their consumption.
  • Set thresholds and receive automatic alerts when exceeding defined budget thresholds
  • View budget history including limits and thresholds, to simplify budget definition and ongoing management.
  • Track spending effortlessly with visual indicators and progress tracking in the UI

When budget thresholds are reached, there are multiple alert options (all options may be used, or you may choose the option which fit best):

1. Visual indicators in the Costs and Usage page, see below.

2. Automatically send alert emails to Global Account Administrators, this is defined when creating a new budget (see email example below).

3. Define custom alerts with SAP Alert Notification service for specific recipients e.g. for subaccount admins or distributions lists (DLs), delivery channels like Microsoft Teams or Slack and more. For more information see: how to enable SAP Alert Notification for SAP BTP and subscribe to budget event: Budget Threshold Exceedance Event (Beta) , see example below.

For more information on Budgets, see documentation

Predict and Optimize Cloud Credit Utilization with Forecasting  

Until now, you could view a monthly graph of your cloud credits trend including actual and estimated costs. With the new Cloud Credits Forecasting capability, you stay ahead using:

  • Cloud Credit Projections: Forecasts monthly costs and usage based on historical consumption data of at least 9 months.
  • Trend Identification: Detects when cloud credits are likely to exceed purchased limits or fall below them, potentially leading to unused credits.
  • Optimized Purchasing: Making informed decisions to adjust credit purchases and reduce the risks of future costs
  • Planning Support: Provide administrators with insights to better plan and manage cloud resources and budgets.

Cloud Credits Forecasting is available for CPEA/BTPEA accounts. The forecast period covers up to 6 months and not beyond the current contract duration. Using this capability will allow you to gain greater control over your global account spending and plan more effectively for future consumption.

For more information see documentation.

Recent Innovations Already Available

While you learn about new capabilities, we would also like to make sure you are aware of already available cost management capabilities, improving transparency and export functionality.

The Costs and Usage page in SAP BTP Cockpit offers a cost breakdown graph by service plans and subaccounts over the last 12 months, enhancing data analysis by highlighting the most expensive subaccounts and service plans. The cost breakdown is available at various levels, including the entire global account, directories, subaccounts, individual services, and any filtered or searched views—such as those using labels. For more information see documentation.

Additionally, we've expanded our export capabilities to support custom data extraction, providing a detailed excel with multiple tailored sheets. This includes exporting filtered data in the UI such as by subaccounts, services, or labels. For detailed cost distribution we have added a new tab in the excel, CF Space Costs by Service, including the monthly cost distribution of service plans across the Cloud Foundry spaces. In addition, we have also added labels as columns for cost distribution-based labels. These capabilities make it easier to perform custom analysis, create pivot tables, and integrate cost data with your existing financial reporting tools. For more information see documentation.

Looking Further Ahead

Beyond Q2 2025, we are continuing to evolve our FinOps capabilities with a strong pipeline of enhancements designed to provide greater control, transparency, and predictability in cloud cost management.

Planned features include expanded budget management to support additional commercial models, and enhanced forecasting capabilities that will enable predictions for cost-related entities across the account hierarchy and services. We are also planning to introduce advanced cross-charging mechanisms through improved tagging/labeling, allowing for more precise cost distribution to lower-level entities. Subaccount administrators will gain deeper cost visibility, empowering them to monitor and manage their spending more effectively. Additionally, advanced monitoring and reporting tools will offer enriched insights to support smarter financial decisions. These innovations will be integrated directly into the SAP BTP Cockpit, delivering a unified, seamless experience for your FinOps activities.

Stay tuned for more details via blogs and roadmap explorer.

 

* This is the current state of planning and may be changed by SAP at any time.​

entry.title: Business partner : Stack of global variables is empty
entry.content:
Hi there,

I am getting the error ' Stack of global variables is empty' while I am switching screens in the BP transaction.

It was working fine earlier, I made some changes in the screen sequence category which I reverted back to what it was like before. 

Please see the attached screenshot.

I will highly appreicate any help on this.

regards

Faraz

entry.title: Mock accesses to a DB Table having a vector field (CAP, node.js)
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using CAP in a node.js project (with  kyma & Hana Cloud, unit tests with Jest).

I have a DB table with embeddings as vectors, stored in HANA. 

To have a good code coverage, I'd like to be able to mock the calls to this table (sqLite cannot be used to have the table locally in an hybrid test profile unfortunately). I haven't found until now any better way to do this than encapsulate my DB accesses in static methods and mock these.

Is there any guidance from CAP on how this should be done? (I did not find one in Capire)

Thanks a lot!

Dewi

entry.title: COMING SOON! Enterprise Asset Management with SAP LearnIX
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION:

 IT Asset Management is critical to an organization’s success. System implementations, upgrades and updates, system design, IT acquisitions and retirements, audits, budgeting, and disaster recovery planning all rely on comprehensive cataloging of not only the IT assets and their attributes, but also their interdependencies and criticality.

This Academic Lesson features SAP LeanIX, an enterprise asset management (EAM) solution, to automate and expedite cataloging company IT assets.  The Learning Journey for this Academic Lesson contains demonstrations and simulations of SAP LeanIX capabilities and provides a demo system for students to explore.  Students will complete the steps of various activities in a simulated mode within the Learning Journey. They will also complete practice exercises within the demo system as part of this Academic Lesson.

SUGGESTED AREAS OF STUDY:

Information systems, solution and enterprise architecture, systems development, project management, computer science, computer engineering, business transformation, enterprise asset management, IT Audit, risk management/assessment.

Appropriate for students at undergraduate and graduate level university.

DOWNLOAD:

Microsoft Word (editable) format: coming soon

PDF (non-editable) format: coming soon

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements: Tree Table (Hierarchy Format)

In this page ( https://software-heroes.com/en/comment/abap-rap-tree-view-en ) you can see one example but they works with O4 and release more resently , I am working with the release 2023 yet.

entry.title: Re: Practice-systems/basic-abap-programming NOT FOUND (404)
entry.content: I am having the same issue. I already opened a ticket for the support and they said they are working on it.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Learning
entry.content: Hello Serigne. Thank you for responding. When I went to the link you provided, it took me to the following Exclusive Content page where there is no content being displayed. I would fall under SAP Partner and do have an activate account.

entry.title: Re: How to filter in the trash in Manager ?
entry.content:
Hi there,

You cannot create a filter that only shows deleted items but the Field Search attribute on filters does include deleted items.  So, you deleted items will be included in the list of items returned when you apply a filter.

If you click on any item in the Trash, there is a Restore button on the top, right corner of Manager.  Click the Restore button to remove the selected item from the Trash and restore it to the content structure.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Validation rule for Asset Master Data

entry.title: Re: Integrating ECC with S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

 

there is quite a lot of information available on the help portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_INTEGRATED_BUSINESS_PLANNING/68fa1e86fe6f41d98421d1ce13a08a9f/a4ed76f3d902423f89cb5899b56cb5a0.html

 

Also looking into the APIs might be useful: https://api.sap.com/

 

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: Physical quantity and available quantity in EWM
entry.content:
Physical quantity is the actual amount you have in the warehouse the stuff you can see and touch.
Available quantity is what you can use or issue, meaning the amount that’s not reserved for any orders or other processes.

For example, if you have 100 items in stock but 20 are reserved for a specific order, then the available quantity is 80.

entry.title: Re: Recap: SAP Sapphire Orlando 2025: Transform your business with SAP BTP Use Cases
entry.content:
Great blog and summary, Joanne! 

Hi everyone, my name is Denise Ma and I am a Senior Product Marketing Specialist for SAP BTP. My days include developing strategic marketing assets and campaigns that drive awareness and adoption of SAP BTP. I translate complex product capabilities into clear, compelling messaging to engage target audiences across various channels and support go-to-market initiatives.

I hope you all enjoyed SAP Sapphire as much as we did!

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️

Thanks for the challenge @ajay_soreng .

Let me just share I thought about the level of difficulty of this challenge.

I find it very simple to solve.

For someone who have never tried CAP is ok, however for anyone else I find it very very basic.

Thanks for your challenge once again.

entry.title: Re: Library view - price and item description
entry.content:
The reason why label Estimated is added next to the price is because you have setting Class Can Override Item Prices enabled. 

Because it is possible for the classes to override the price, we show the estimated label - if it is turned off, it should show the price without the estimated value.

The estimated price appears because classes can vary in price. While an item has a set base price, the multiple classes created from that same item may have varying prices.

3511612 - New Learning Experience: Enhanced Search

The KBA 2597029 that you mentioned is only applicable to old legacy library view.

The only way to hide 'Estimated' text from showing it new Library Search is to disabled the setting 'Class Can Override Item Prices' on an item level i.e. by navigating to LMS-> Learning Activities-> Search for item that has price-> Go to Pricing tab-> Uncheck the setting 'Class Can Override Item Prices' on an item level.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Learning

Regards

entry.title: How to raise priority of a support case when it’s in Customer Action in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Your support case is in customer action, and you need a guide on how to change the priority? This SAP Blog is for you!

Managing support cases efficiently is crucial, especially if it's in "Customer Action" status. Understanding how to navigate the SAP Support Portal (SAP for Me) can be crucial for timely resolutions and maintaining business continuity.

Before diving into the steps, it's essential to understand what "Customer Action" means. This status indicates that the support case requires input or action from the customer before SAP can proceed with further investigation or resolution. The support team might need more details to proceed with the case. This could include logs, screenshots, or specific error messages, or you may be asked to implement a suggested solution or workaround to see if it resolves the issue, etc.

 

Here's a step-by-step guide on how to change the priority of a support case in SAP for Me when the status is “Customer Action”.

 

  1. Login to SAP for Me using your SAP Universal ID or S-user ID & Password
  2. Under “Dashboards”, click on “Services & Support”.
  3. Click on “View Cases”.
  4. Open the case from your Case List. 
  5. Click the Edit (pencil icon) beside the Priority.
  6. In the Priority/Business Impact drop down, select the new priority (e.g. High).
  7. Fill out the required details, e.g. detailed business impact statement, & click on “Submit”.
  8. Click on the “Reply” button and provide the necessary information. If there are any attachments that need to be included, kindly upload them to the Attachments tab for easy reference (see KBA  How to add an attachment to a support case - SAP for Me.)
  9. Lastly, click on “Send” after providing all the required information. 

Note: Before increasing the priority, please refer to KBA: 67739 - Priority of problem cases - SAP for Me to know more about the 4 priority definitions (Low, Medium, High, Very High). Increasing the priority of a support case can be necessary when the issue at hand significantly impacts your business operations or poses a risk to critical processes.

 

Additionally, it’s mandatory that you provide detailed business impact information to speed up the processing of your case. Increasing the priority of a support case in SAP for Me requires clear communication and thorough documentation. See KBA: 1281633 - Speed Up Processing of a Case - SAP for Me. By following these steps, you can ensure that your case receives the attention it needs from SAP.

Remember, the key is to provide SAP with all the necessary information to understand the urgency and impact of the issue.

 

Reminder:

Some Product Areas or Components do not allow High or Very High priority. If you click the Change Priority button, and the dropdown shows only Low or Medium, that means that specific area does not allow the priority to be raised.

For Product/Component areas that do allow a change in priority, you can only change the priority if the case has one of the following statuses:

  • Not Sent to SAP (saved as draft)
  • Customer Action
  • Partner-Customer Action
  • SAP Proposed Solution

It is not possible to change the priority when the case status is any of the following. The Change Priority button will not be displayed.

  • Sent to SAP
  • Sent to SAP Partner
  • In Processing by SAP
  • Confirmed
  • Confirmed Automatically

If needed, contact the Customer Interaction Center (CIC) for assistance and guidance.  KBA: 560499 - Customer Interaction Center: Hotline - Email - Chat

 

Helpful KBAs & Links:

KBA: 2554794- How to update a case that is in Customer Action - SAP for Me

KBA: 1522544 - How to change the priority of a support case - SAP for Me

KBA: 2403394 - How to change support case details - SAP for Me

SAP BLOG: How to Prioritize and Speed Up the Processing of y... - SAP Community

 

Got More Questions?

Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have!  

Alternatively, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A  All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

 

Don't Miss Out on Free Webcasts

We would like to invite you to our free webcasts, which we believe can help you get the best from your support in SAP. You can find all upcoming webcasts on https://events.sap.com/supportwebcasts/en/home. These sessions are open to all users and will revolve around hot topics in support today such as SAP for Me, Case Management, Universal ID, etc. Additionally, you can explore a range of CIC how-to videos at learning.sap.com. Please feel free to share this information with your colleagues. We are looking forward to your attendance.

 

Lastly, you may want to access all SAP CIC Blogs here:  https://community.sap.com/t5/tag/CustomerInteractionCenter/tg-p/board-id/crm-blog-sap

 

Thanks for reading! We hope you found this guide helpful.

entry.title: Re: Colgate's Journey with SAP BTP: Preserving HR Admin Functionality from SAP HCM to SAP SuccessFac
entry.content: This is the latest in a series of webinars that discuss the application of SAP's Business Technology Platform to SuccessFactors HCM customers; I highly recommend it for the opportunity to hear from an actual customer who is using both and to learn more about joint HCM + BTP use cases.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products and SAP BusinessObjects: Similar Purpose, Different Approa
entry.content:
" ... Is list of predefined bdc data products published anywhere? ..."
+1 😁

I have not yet seen any presentation on how such a ‘Data Product’ works in detail
and/or which tables are exactly used for this
and how we can extend it, as this is probably only the basic data, similar to a BW extractor.

entry.title: Deep Dive into SAP BDC Data products
entry.content:
In my earlier 2 blogs I have talked about overview of SAP BDC and its key features.

SAP BDC for Dummies - SAP Community

SAP BDC Key Features - SAP Community

In this blog we will continue to deep dive in SAP BDC key feature Data Products.

Deep Dive into SAP BDC Data products

What are Data products in SAP BDC?

Data Products in SAP Business Data Cloud serve as a standardized and efficient way to share and consume data across applications and domains. They enable analytic scenarios, AI applications, and facilitate data integration while being optimized for intensive reads. Managed with a product mindset, they are supported by high-quality metadata and governed by decentralized ownership principles.

Data Product is a grouping concept that carries the relevant metadata to describe the dataset and its data qualities. It may consists of multiple objects.

A data product is a self-contained set of tables that has data exposed for consumption outside the producing application or service via APIs.

SAP applications such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud produce data products that can be consumed in SAP Datasphere via SAP Business Data Cloud data packages. Data products are also included in and provide the data for consumption by SAP Business Data Cloud intelligent applications.

A Data Product itself is not directly consumable, but it exposes data through output ports, which can be implemented as APIs or events.

A data product provides a predefined and stable structure and also the inbound and outbound interfaces for source system extraction and end tool consumption.

Data products are created in and only available in SAP Datasphere.

Basically, a Data product acts like a Datasource that can, not-only pull data from your sources but also has the required structure to store it, load capabilities and can help consume this data across various intelligent applications.

When an Intelligent Application is installed, it often creates one or more Data Products by pulling in raw business data from source systems, transforming and organizing it during the setup process. During this process the data extraction, harmonization, and integration is taken care by Data products.

 

Key Objectives​

  1. Efficient Data Sharing Across Domains: Data Products facilitate replication-free integration and consumption by SAP, customer, and partner applications via APIs and Events.
  2. Efficient Data Storage: Data Products are processed and curated in a modern object-store based data lake architecture with highly scalable Spark processing.
  3. High-Quality Metadata: Metadata includes business semantics and is accessible through directories like the Data Product Directory and ORD Aggregators, enhancing discoverability.
  4. Optimized for Analytics: Data sets are curated for intensive reads and consumed in a read-only fashion, ensuring efficiency and reliability.
  5. Supporting Analytical and AI Applications: Data Products provide foundational data for dashboards, analytical models, and AI applications.
  6. Decentralized Ownership: Inspired by Data Mesh principles, Data Products are owned and managed by domain experts or teams responsible for operational data.
  7. Lifecycle Management: Data Products undergo needs analysis, design, and delivery phases, ensuring their accuracy and relevance.

Types

A data product can be of the type:

  • Primary: A data product exposed for consumption directly from an application. It's likely not to have input ports.
  • Derived: A data product that aggregates and combines data from multiple sources to provide value-added data sets. Derived data products must provide at least one input port with an integration dependency with at least one requirement.

Categories

Based on the dataset associated with the data product, it can have the following categories:

  • Business objects: The dataset contains business objects of type master data and transactional data.
  • Analytical data: The dataset contains analytical data such as KPIs, cubes, and metrics.
  • Other: All other types of datasets such as configuration data, mixed, and so on.

What are Data Product Definition files?

 Data Product Definition files are structured artifacts that describe and encapsulate curated datasets—either master or transactional data—designed for easy, secure, and reusable consumption across applications and domains. These files define the metadata, structure, and access interfaces of a data product.

These files are built using Open Resource Discovery (ORD) standards, ensuring consistency and interoperability.

Stored within Foundation Services, these definition files play a key role in discovering required data products.

As we continue our journey with SAP BDC we hope to discover more usage of Data products and how they can be utilized in the best way possible.

 

Happy Learning!

 

Swati Gawade-Shingade.

Cognizant technology Solutions.

entry.title: S/4 HANA Private Cloud Extensibility - Manage Reservation Items
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I am looking to extend the standard SAP FIORI App - Manage Reservation Items. Specifically, I would like to add a new filter based on the MARC table (Custom Z field) to filter items records  and a button for goods movement. I would greatly appreciate your suggestions on how to approach this while adhering to the clean core methodology.

Do I need to extend App using Custom Fields App or directly extending the underlying CDS View.

Thank you very much for your assistance.

Best regards,

Sandeep

entry.title: Tariffs in Supply Chain Planning
entry.content:
Understanding The Tariff Turbulence

 

Now that the initial shock of recent tariff shifts and the wild claims about tariff solutions have somewhat abated, it’s time to apply some rational thought to the topic and how it may or may not impact supply chains in general and specifically, supply chain planning. It’s important to understand what tariffs are, how they are applied and in what sphere of concern they reside. It is equally important to understand how technology may or may not be able to address the core truths and impacts of tariffs.

The foundation of tariffs begins with the simple definition we all know. Tariffs are a fee or surcharge levied by governing bodies for the privilege of bringing some product manufactured in a different sovereign nation into the nation levying the tariff. This becomes a part of the overall cost of the product.

We have always had tariffs of some type. They are not new. What is new is the size and volatility of the tariffs.  These fees historically are only a small part of the overall cost of importing a product and as such, did not require any specific considerations from a planning and supply perspective. In our current reality, tariff application is broader and much more substantial than before, in many cases equaling or exceeding the actual acquisition cost of the product. Part of the dilemma facing companies as it applies to tariffs is the volatility and instability of the tariff structure. How long are we going to have them in their current state? Are they going to go away? How often and how large are the changes going to be? Currently, tariffs pose a significant threat to margins and profitability and so the simple tariff has become a disruption to continued business for many companies.

If we can agree that tariffs now present themselves as a potential disruption to existing supply chains, we should probably talk about disruptions in general. Disruptions are any internal or external action or event that disturbs the demand/supply balance and inhibits the ability of a company to profitably deliver to customers on-time-in-full. Supply chain disruptions are not new, they are an inevitability. Some are small, some large. Some are local and some are global. Some are self-imposed/self-inflicted, and some are totally outside of a company’s control. Regardless of size, scale or scope, disruptions are inevitable. Today’s tariffs are just another disruption.

So, what mechanism exists to deal with disruption? Wild and varied claims have been touted about newly developed capabilities that have been magically built overnight or that by sprinkling a little AI pixie dust on tariff volatility somehow the dilemma is solved. Taking a deep breath and stepping off the hype train, and then applying just a little critical thought one quickly realizes that those claims are just that, claims, and that those claims don’t really pass the sniff test.  The planning framework that has historically been used to understand the impact of disruptions to a business is a process known as S&OP or Sales and Operations Planning. S&OP is, by definition, a collaborative, iterative, alignment process, aligning a company’s resources to achieve company goals. It may also be known by its more mature name, Integrated Business Planning (I generally use those names interchangeably).

One of the main tactical goals of S&OP is to help a company fulfil its obligations to its customers at the lowest reasonable price, preserving margins and protecting market share. One of the ways that it does that is through a planning activity often called scenario planning, also known as what-if planning or simulations. It takes the existing business plan and, separate from that plan, allows planners and analysts to interactively change values that affect the business plan and measure and compare the outcomes on a variety of dimensions. An inconvenient truth is that AI cannot do that as there is no training dataset that can teach AI what to look at, what to change and what the decision tree looks like.

Now let’s look at tariffs specifically as a disruption. As previously mentioned, tariffs are not new. What’s different is that, historically for the most part, tariffs have been a relatively small part of the overall cost of a product and, as a cost element, did not figure into disruptions. In today’s volatile and unpredictable arena, tariffs have become a major consideration and, in many cases, exceed the total cost of the product itself. This forces companies to examine their plans and consider sourcing alternatives. The way they systemically do that is by creating scenarios in their S&OP or IBP process that incorporate tariffs as a decision lever and then analyzing and comparing those scenarios. This is a cost and profitability approach that provides baseline data for decisions but is not the only dimension that needs to be considered.

Configured correctly, optimizers can scrutinize a company’s digital twin and using Machine Learning logic (OK, I concede certain AI tools can be effectively used) can rationalize the supply plan based on cost. Tariff relevant values can be changed in separate scenarios and then optimized and the then results compared. However, this is strictly a mathematical exercise.

Once the cost impact is known, a company must determine and decide whether to absorb the tariff, resulting in a hit to margins and profitability or increase selling prices to protect margins. Perhaps the decision is a little of both, hedging short term profitability to retain market share. They must also recognize and model the possible impact to overall demand as prices may push customers to not buy or to delay buying. Threats to market share and company image and reputation must also be represented in the scenario process. These are qualitative decisions, not necessarily quantitative and as such require human wisdom.

In the near-term, there is another dimension to planning that must be included in this process. How does any of the scenarios modelled impact your suppliers.  In this area we all may need to change our way of thinking a little. All our suppliers have capacity limitations themselves. Our suppliers also have history with us, and they allocate some of their capacity to our historical demand and/or forecasts that we provide. As we model different scenarios with tariff-driven outcomes we need to have the ability to digitally connect with our suppliers and quickly and iteratively send them potential forecast(s) and receive their soft commits in return – not in a point-to-point manner, or through even more cumbersome methods such as email.  The velocity and volume of these data exchanges requires a one-to-many digital business network to succeed. Those soft commits will either validate or invalidate those scenarios and become a part of the decision process. This positions our trading partners as an integral part of our planning process.

Finally, in the frenzy to react to these financial pressure points caused by these extreme tariffs it is necessary to acknowledge that permanent solutions cannot be quickly achieved. These are strategic, capital planning level alternatives that need to be modeled and compared in scenarios. These alternatives may include options to onshore or nearshore to a more favorable tariff source, to build internal capabilities or to refurbish existing capabilities. All these options carry significant risk and expense and given the volatility and unknown future of the tariffs, create tough decisions for companies.

In simple summary, the ability to easily create alternative options through the use of imbedded scenarios, leverage algorithmic machine learning logic to perform the complex mathematical calculations, and compare the outcomes of those scenarios in an easily consumable manner are the keys to resilient supply chain planning and getting our companies through this latest disruption to our carefully laid plans. Contact your SAP representative to explore SAP solutions that assist in weathering the Tariff Turbulence.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2023, Fully-Activated Appliance on Google Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Am deploying SAP S/4HANA 2023, Fully-Activated Appliance on Google Cloud with below results:

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Fully-Activated Appliance - successful ✅

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, Fully-Activated Appliance - software error 🛑

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03, Fully-Activated Appliance - software error 🛑

Since my deployment of 2023FPS01 is successful seems my Google Cloud project looks good.

But any advise on why 2023FPS02 and 2023FPS03 got software error when deployed?

Please kindly advise.

Thanks.

entry.title: Issue in Service Layer's Script Engine (.... has been blocked by CORS policy.)
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am using SAP HANA Service and Script Engine with flutter web application.
When I call the Service Layer API, I used withCredentials=true so the dio save cookies set by response header.
I can use all types of Service Layer API and it works.
But when I try to use Script Engine API in my code it give error related to CORS.
The Script Engine URL
/b1s/v1/script/{{partner name}}/{{script name}}

The Error:
localhost/:1 Access to XMLHttpRequest at 'https://hanaserver:50000/b1s/v1/script/ABC/ReceiveFG' from origin 'http://localhost:8080' has been blocked by CORS policy: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header is present on the requested resource.

If I set withCredentials=false in my application, then all Service Layer API get fails and flutter application also does not allow to set B1SessionID cookie in the request.

Does anyone have any solution for this?

Thank you.

Manish

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
HI @David_Chaviano, 

Is a postgraduate course valid for this program?

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content: Well done team, I look forward to an improved user friendly UI experience.

entry.title: Re: Are there step by step guidelines in learning HUB for...
entry.content: Also, There are few YouTube Video which can help you to have basic overview of SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: Timesheet Module Behavior on Attendance and Leave Col...
entry.content:
Hello,

Wish everything is fine on your end. I suppose the collision error is logged when you were importing time data to time sheet via External Time Data, right ?

I can understand you may expect such collision not to block the import of external time data of other days that do not have this collision issue, but currently it is the design that the collision within the time sheet period will block the whole time sheet period, to avoid any further data inconsistency. 

This collision check and error handling is currently hard-coded, so no possibility currently available to get over this.

However actually from business perspective to avoid unnecessary data inconsistency risks, it is recommended to clear the collision data first. 
Actually, the fact is, any import error will block the whole time sheet period from any further data importing via External Time Data. The reason behind is very much the same.

And to monitor the import errors, Admin Alerts or the Administer Time workbench are always the very direct tool to monitor and correct the business data based on your business fact.

Best regards.

Lucy

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content: Great to see the brand new page design, very user-friendly.

entry.title: Key User Extensibility: Add custom field without adding the field to underlying DB table
entry.content:
For my use-case, I need to be able to add a custom field to the signature of a Business Context (eg. PO header MM_PURDOC_HEADER) so that the field is available across the layers through into BADIs, but I do not need to save the field value into the database. It will be more like a "calculation field" that will ultimately drive the values in the fields that actually are on the DB table.

Is this possible with Key User Extensibility? I've tried with our S/4HANA on-prem 2023 FPS 02 but didn't see any option to do this. Any custom field I add with the KUT ends up being added to the DB table.

Or do I need to use developer extensibility to achieve this?

entry.title: Re: Shift Scenarios in SAP SF Time Tracking
entry.content:
Hello,

Wish everything is good on your end. Regarding your described situation, where the system always captures the recorded time based on the assigned work schedule, it is the correct system behaviour.

I can understand that your employees may work "flexibly" against the assigned work schedule, but the key point is the system cannot know how the employee is flexibly working differently from the assigne work schedule, right ? So the system can only associate the recording/punch times based on the existing work schedule assigned to this employee.

 

But to avoid this, we have a new feature delivered with 2H 2024 release, where with TTR enabled, employees can assign themselves Temporary Time Information to overwrite the original planned working time even in ESS scenario. 
Create Temporary Work Schedule Employee Self-Service UI  
Temporary Holiday Calendar 
It supports assigning him/herself a temporary work schedule to overwrite the original one, and it also supports assigning him/herself a holiday. 

So for your situation, I would like to propose consideration on above new feature, and to ensure the employee assigns him/herself the temporary work schedule in ESS before s/he records time or punches in/out. 
In this way, the recorded time and punch times will follow the new work schedule.

 

So please understand, the system can never know how the planned working time is, until it is persisted as work schedule or Temporary Time Information for the employee. So it can only capture the recorded time and punch times based on what it knows.
To make the system know the change, please ensure to tell it before hand, via for example Temporary Time Information assignment as above. And then the recorded time and punch times will be captured based on the new planned working time.

Please understand, currently if you assign TTI or change planned working time after the time or punch times have been recorded, even recalculation will not re-capture the records. This is a known restriction now, and we are working on possibility to improve this one. KBA 3616157 is published for this symptom for customer awareness too.

Wish above helps.

Best regards.

Lucy

entry.title: Re: MTS Scenario to have multiple Purchase Req. for same...
entry.content: Thanks @Prabha_A , we were able to propose this solution on Lot size to the client who were expecting MTO like process flow in a MTS scenario (doesn't seem feasible in the standard )

entry.title: Re: EC Alerts and Notifications
entry.content:
Hi there, 

Does anyone know if employee's number can be brought into EC Alert message? 

Have tried all these variations of tags but none works. 

[[SUBJECT_USER_ID]]

[[USER_ID]]

[[EMPLOYEE_ID]]

[[USERID]]

[[USER_NAME]]

[[empId]]

[[PERSON_ID]]

 

Appreciate it!

entry.title: Customer Acknowledgement of goods received after booking sales invoice (VF01)
entry.content:
Hi All,

There is a requirement from client in which once goods are dispatched & sales invoice is booked (VF01), client wants to have a process of performing acknowledgement that the customer has received the goods.

I am aware of the process of POD as seen in the below blog.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-members/pod-proof-of-delivery/ba-p/13220429

But in POD case, sales invoice is not generated if POD is yet to be completed.

The client wants the sales invoice & Einvoicing to be generated & only after that should there be a customer acknowledgement step purely for reporting purposes.

 

Is there a standard way in SAP to only perform acknowledgement (similar to VLPOD) after VF01?

 

Regards,

Gavin.

entry.title: Deliv.Rel. Billg Sts field in SO
entry.content:
I would like to get this field in SO via CDS views.

 

Is there any CDS views which include Deliv.Rel. Billg Sts field?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Re: use case for "Application.getfiledatasource" ?
entry.content:
Hi @DenisT ,

Thanks for your solution!
However, I still don't understand where the modelId is obtained from. My understanding is that it should be the technical name of a model taken from the Data Source to serve as the modelId. But in the logs, the information indicates an incorrect parameter setting, so I'm unclear about what's happening. Do you have more specific details? Looking forward to your response!

Best Regards,
Wenjun

entry.title: Re: How to create the infoobject of Key figure with +/- s...
entry.content: Nice job !

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Orders - Automatic Extraction (II): One-Click Setup for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @SS10465 ,

For S/4HANA 2023 FSP02, please refer to the Set-up instructions for scope item 04X9: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-055/2023-FPS02/SolP/4X9?region=DE

Best Regards,

Robyn

entry.title: How to monitor AIF errors in delta way from SAP Solman 7.2
entry.content:
Hi, 

We have a requirement to monitor AIF error messages in delta way from sap solution manager, example how many AIF errors occurred for particular interface in last hour?

Also, apart from standard filters like namespace, aif interface name, interface version; /aif/err has additional filters how do we provide those filters in SolMan?

Additional filters:

Kindly help me with your suggestions and recommendations.

Regards,

Vivek

entry.title: Account Assignment P - Goods receipt for Project Orders with material code for stock
entry.content:
We are trying to do a Goods Receipt of Account Assignment P – Purchase Order WITH MATERIAL CODE and we could see that the material is getting consumed on the WBS but is not generating inventory for processing.

 Business wants  to use Project PO for stock .  Can you please advise how we can implement the requirement.

entry.title: Re: Employment Status in Story reports
entry.content:
Hi @Punita ,

"Status" field under Basic User Information schema  is greyed out.

---->Under Manage Business configuration -> Employee Profile --> Standard -> Status, please check if the object is defined correctly, like Enabled should be "Yes".

If the config is correct, please check if permission is maintained for status field under Employee data in RBP

Regards,

Subbu

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC Free Trial Version
entry.content: Yes, it was released a few weeks back around Sapphire . Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: Bottle neck issue because of the highest number of display hits in VIM Central workplace
entry.content:
Hi ,

I'm facing a bottle neck issue for a specific user for the tcode /otx/pf03_wp as the user has selected the display hits as 999999. 

Any inputs on troubleshooting the issue as we are not able to change the display hits, when we logout and login in takes the display hits as default and starts to run the tcode and end up in the resource bottle neck error.

 

Any inputs on how to change the value will be helpful.

Thanks.

entry.title: How to add REVLV field in ME2L/ME2M Report
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

How to add REVLV field in ME2L and ME2M Report.

entry.title: Re: the billing staus of sales order item in the CDS
entry.content:
Hello,

Yes, this is the system behavior.

If you want to get detail information of status, please open the view of "Status Overview". See screenshot below.

Best regards,

Yong

entry.title: Re: Open Journal Entry App in a New Tab from P&L or G/L R...

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing 25.02 Release enhancements and new capabilities
entry.content:
Hi Manoel,

Is the Publish MQTT Message service is still valid or has it been removed in the last upgrade. Because we are not able to find it or do we need any special role assignments for this?

Could you please help on this?

Regard's

Uday.
